# Alembic migrations

The DB URL is read at runtime from `Settings.database_url` (env `DATABASE_URL`),
so nothing secret lives in `alembic.ini`. The environment is async and works with
any async driver.

## Common commands

Run from `backend/`:

```bash
# Apply all migrations (production: Postgres via asyncpg)
alembic upgrade head

# Create a new autogenerated migration after changing models
alembic revision --autogenerate -m "describe change"

# Roll back one revision
alembic downgrade -1

# Show current / history
alembic current
alembic history
```

## Notes

- `env.py` imports `app.models` so autogenerate sees the full schema.
- Enum columns are stored as `VARCHAR + CHECK` (portable, migration-friendly).
- The initial migration was generated and reviewed against the ORM metadata; apply
  it to a fresh Postgres database with `alembic upgrade head`.
